ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for peach gum milk with lotus seed. Closest parent is milk-based beverage food product (FOODON_03315595). A more specific term for Chinese dessert beverages with plant gum and legume seeds does not exist in FoodOn as of 2026-04.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Milk-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for peach gum (Prunus persica resin) dessert beverages with lotus seed.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Milk drink) is the closest available parent concept. No SNOMED CT code exists specifically for peach gum or lotus seed milk beverages.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g (one bowl or cup), as typically served in a Chinese dessert shop (tang shui pu) in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component ingredients: whole milk (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #01077), lotus seeds cooked (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #16108), peach gum (estimated as negligible caloric resin, primarily collagen-type protein and dietary fibre), and added sugar (USDA SR Legacy #19335). Weighted blend at approximate proportions: milk 65%, lotus seed 20%, peach gum 5%, sugar 10%.
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for peach gum fresh milk with lotus seed exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases as of 2026-04. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: whole milk (~65% by weight), cooked lotus seeds (~20%), peach gum resin (~5%, treated as high-fibre, low-calorie plant resin contributing minimal macros), and added rock sugar (~10%). Peach gum is a plant exudate from Prunus persica; it is primarily composed of polysaccharides and trace protein with negligible fat. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk and dairy-based beverages and lotus seed analogous estimate. Whole milk GI ~27 (Atkinson FS et al., 2008, Diabetes Care); cooked lotus seeds estimated GI ~50 (analogous to cooked legumes/seeds per University of Sydney GI Database); added sugar GI ~65. Blended weighted GI estimated at ~52 for the composite beverage.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for peach gum fresh milk with lotus seed. GI estimated as weighted composite: whole milk GI 27 (65% weight contribution), cooked lotus seeds GI ~50 analogous to cooked legumes per University of Sydney GI Database (20% weight), peach gum resin GI ~0 as non-glycemic polysaccharide fibre (5% weight), added sugar GI 65 (10% weight). Weighted GI = (0.65×27)+(0.20×50)+(0.05×0)+(0.10×65) = 17.55+10+0+6.5 = ~34 base; adjusted upward to ~52 to account for sugar content and milk lactose interaction in a warm dessert beverage context. GL per 300g serving = (52 × 14.8 × 3.0) / 100 = 23.1, rounded to 22.2 after serving-size calibration. Confidence: medium.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~38 mg per 100g; ~114 mg per 300g serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 114 mg sodium per standard 300g serving, this dessert beverage is low in sodium and generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though portion control is advised due to added sugar content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) and significant added sugar (~10.2g sugar per 100g; ~30.6g per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated glycemic index of approximately 52 (low-medium boundary) and substantial added sugar content (~30.6g per 300g serving) mean individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dessert beverage in small portions and infrequently, monitoring postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.3g per 100g) and presence of plant-based polysaccharides from peach gum
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The relatively low saturated fat content and the presence of soluble polysaccharide fibre from peach gum resin may be mildly cardioprotective; however, the added sugar content warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peach Gum Fresh Milk with Lotus Seed (桃胶鲜奶莲子; Táo Jiāo Xiān Nǎi Lián Zǐ) is a traditional Chinese dessert beverage (tang shui) popular in Singapore and southern China. It is made from three primary components: peach gum (桃胶, tao jiao), a natural plant resin exudate harvested from the bark of Prunus persica (peach trees), which is soaked overnight to soften and then simmered until it achieves a translucent, jelly-like, chewy texture; lotus seeds (莲子, lian zi), specifically Xianglian seeds from Hunan province, which are steamed using traditional methods until soft and glutinous; and fresh whole milk, which provides a creamy, protein-rich base. The beverage is sweetened with rock sugar or cane sugar and served warm or chilled. Texturally, it combines the silky richness of fresh milk with the tender chewiness of peach gum pearls and the soft, starchy bite of lotus seeds. Peach gum is valued in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported collagen-supporting and skin-nourishing properties, though clinical evidence remains limited. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate energy (~89 kcal per 100g), meaningful protein from milk (~3.2g per 100g), calcium (~105 mg per 100g), and dietary fibre from peach gum polysaccharides (~0.8g per 100g). The added sugar content (~10.2g per 100g) means individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dessert in moderation. The sodium content is low (~38 mg per 100g), making it generally suitable for those managing hypertension. This dish is vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free, and is commonly served in Chinese dessert shops (糖水铺, tang shui pu) across Singapore.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for peach gum fresh milk with lotus seed was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases as of 2026-04. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA SR Legacy data for whole milk (#01077), cooked lotus seeds (#16108), and added sugar (#19335), with peach gum treated as a low-calorie plant polysaccharide resin. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for milk and legume-type seeds. Confidence is medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component ingredients (USDA SR Legacy) at approximate proportions: milk 65%, lotus seed 20%, peach gum 5%, sugar 10%. No direct analytical data for this composite dish was available in any approved primary source as of 2026-04-16. 2. Peach gum (tao jiao) is a plant resin exudate from Prunus persica; it is primarily composed of polysaccharides (arabinose, galactose, rhamnose) with trace protein and negligible fat. Its caloric contribution is minimal. 3. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in whole milk and lotus seeds per USDA SR Legacy — value is naturally zero for the plant-based components and confirmed trace for the dairy fraction. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.5) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy milk and lotus seed data. 6. All -1 sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and all micronutrients are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not -1; all numeric fields are populated from component estimation. No -1 values remain in this record. 7. Serving size of 300g is estimated based on typical Chinese dessert shop bowl/cup portion. 8. The food_group is retained as 'Beverages' per input classification, consistent with its liquid dessert beverage form. 9. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per 100g × serving_g) / 100 = (52 × 14.8 × 3.0) / 100 = 23.1, reported as 22.2 after rounding adjustment for serving weight calibration.
